carriedBehavior: The slash command executes immediately and returns a grouped health report instead of routing through the turn loop.

The report is typed as findings with severity, category, summary counts, details, and remediation text, matching the donor shape of grouped diagnostic findings while rendering in AgenC's text-only slash command surface.
Runtime checks cover Node.js version, working directory existence, AgenC home/config-store availability, and built package artifacts from the LP-22 build contract.
Daemon checks use the F-03i pid/socket/cookie paths and pid liveness helper, reporting missing pid, stale pid, missing socket, bad socket path, and daemon cookie permission state.

Provider checks use the A-01/A-03 provider discovery and auth backend surfaces to evaluate the active session provider/model, BYOK or managed-key availability, subscription errors, and auth identity status.

Sandbox checks exercise the C-01d platform transform path through SandboxManager on Linux or macOS, validate the packaged Linux helper or seatbelt executable, and run the existing bubblewrap capability probe.

MCP checks use the MS-01 session MCP manager facade, effective server map, connected server list, and required-server flags to distinguish disabled, connected, disconnected optional, and disconnected required servers.
intentionalReductions: The React/Ink doctor screen is not ported; AgenC's current slash command contract is text output, so the grouped report renderer lives in runtime/src/diagnostics/doctor.ts.

Installer, update, keybinding, package-manager, and global binary checks from the donor diagnostics are not carried because OB-04's item spec replaces them with AgenC daemon, provider, sandbox, MCP, and runtime artifact health checks.
Context-warning donors are folded into AgenC-owned runtime/config/artifact checks. No donor memory-file naming or prompt-surface warning text is retained.
The donor MCP CLI subcommand is folded into /doctor rather than adding a separate agenc mcp doctor command in this item; runtime/src/commands/mcp.ts keeps the existing MCP status command.

commands: npm exec --workspace=@tetsuo-ai/runtime vitest run src/diagnostics/doctor.test.ts src/commands/command-surface.test.ts
